model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: Purdue is a top-10 team in both KenPom and Torvik with an elite offense (top-2 nationally) and a solid defensive baseline, while Queens profiles as a ~180–200-level team whose defense grades very poorly (KenPom AdjD ~117; Torvik defensive rank outside the top 300). That mismatch should show up in shot quality and efficiency over 40 minutes, and the market/ESPN analytics also imply a near-certain Purdue advance (Purdue -25.5; ~98% win probability). Queens’ credible upset path is almost entirely extreme three-point variance plus Purdue turnovers, but Queens’ defensive profile makes it hard to generate enough stops to capitalize.

reasoningStep.title: West R64 (2/15): Purdue over Queens
reasoningStep.summary: Purdue’s elite offense and large overall efficiency gap make this a low-upset-risk 2/15 matchup.
evidence: Ratings: Purdue KenPom #8 (AdjEM +30.5; AdjO 131 #2; AdjD 100.5) and Torvik #8 (AdjO #1; AdjD #36) vs Queens KenPom #181 (AdjEM -1.45; AdjO 115.8 #78; AdjD 117.3) and Torvik #199 (AdjO #85; AdjD #316).
Market/forecast: ESPN lists Purdue around a 97.8% win probability with Purdue favored by ~25.5 points (ML about -8000), consistent with the large efficiency mismatch.
Matchup note: Queens’ offense is respectable for a mid-major, but its defense is the glaring weakness, which is a bad fit against Purdue’s high-efficiency scoring.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
